skill.name: project-planner
skill.description: Generate structured project planning documents for software projects, including requirements, system design, and implementation tasks. The skill provides templates, domain patterns, and optional local scripts for creating and validating Markdown planning artifacts.
skill.summary: Create requirements, design, and task plans for software projects.
skill.author: Dwsy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but review found most are false positives from Markdown fences, metadata URLs, and placeholder security text. The confirmed risk is limited to local scripts that write generated Markdown files and read user-specified validation files. No prompt injection, command execution, runtime network access, or credential exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Local File Write Without Output Path Guardrails
medium_findings.description: The document generator creates the requested output directory and writes requirements.md, design.md, and tasks.md. This is expected behavior for the skill, but a user-selected output path could overwrite existing planning files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly creates directories and writes files with open(..., "w"). The behavior is local and transparent, so the concern is overwrite scope rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: User-Specified Local File Reads in Validator
medium_findings.description: The validator accepts document paths from command-line arguments and reads those files for analysis. This is legitimate validation behavior, but users should avoid pointing it at sensitive local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parser exposes path arguments and the validator reads each path directly. No network transmission or command execution was found, which limits severity.

low_findings.title: Template Credential Placeholders Are Not Real Secret Access
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged database and API key strings, but these are placeholder values inside generated documentation templates. No evidence found that the script reads real environment variables or harvests credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The strings are embedded in a Markdown template and use placeholder values such as your-api-key. There is no runtime env access at these lines.

low_findings.title: Static Network Indicators Are Metadata or Documentation
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs in plugin metadata and API wording in templates are not runtime network calls. No evidence found of HTTP client imports, outbound requests, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The plugin entries are homepage and repository metadata, and the reference file contains project-planning text. Search confirmed no runtime network library usage in the scripts.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command findings come from Markdown code fences and usage examples. They document how to run the provided scripts and are not executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears in documentation blocks rather than executable control flow. The Python scripts do not call subprocess, os.system, eval, or exec.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Write Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The generator writes Markdown output files to a caller-selected directory using os.makedirs and open in write mode.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file creation and write operations are explicit. The pattern is expected for document generation but requires user awareness.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Read Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The validator reads local files supplied through command-line path arguments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI arguments map directly to open calls. The data stays local unless another tool handles it later.

content.user_title: Plan Software Projects With Structured Docs
content.value_statement: Software projects often start with unclear requirements, missing design details, and weak task traceability. This skill turns an idea into requirements, architecture, and implementation plans that AI coding tools can follow.
actual_capabilities: Creates requirements documents with user stories and acceptance criteria.
Generates system design sections for components, data flows, APIs, and deployment.
Builds implementation plans with phases, tasks, dependencies, and traceability.
Provides domain templates for trading, ecommerce, SaaS, ML, developer tools, and other systems.
Includes optional scripts to generate and validate Markdown planning files.
limitations: Generated documents require project-specific review and completion.
The scripts create templates; they do not design a production system automatically.
Validation checks structure and consistency, not technical correctness.
Local scripts can overwrite generated document names in the selected output directory.

use_cases.title: Define a New Product Scope
use_cases.description: Turn a product idea into user stories, acceptance criteria, boundaries, and measurable success criteria.

use_cases.title: Prepare an AI Coding Build Plan
use_cases.description: Create design and task documents that guide Claude, Codex, or Claude Code during implementation.

use_cases.title: Standardize Technical Planning
use_cases.description: Use consistent templates for requirements, architecture, deployment, testing, and task traceability across projects.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Project Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new software idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create requirements, design, and task documents for a small web app that helps teams track project decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Add Constraints and Users
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a SaaS analytics project for three user roles. Include scope boundaries, non-functional requirements, and task phases.

prompt_templates.title: Generate an Architecture-First Specification
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for system design work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a project architect for an API service. Define components, data flows, integration points, API contracts, risks, and implementation tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Existing Planning Documents
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for an existing project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current requirements, design, and tasks. Find missing traceability, unclear boundaries, weak acceptance criteria, and incomplete dependencies.

output_examples.input: Plan a customer support dashboard for a small SaaS team.
output: A requirements document with user roles, support workflows, and acceptance criteria.
A design document with dashboard components, ticket data flow, integrations, and deployment notes.
An implementation plan with phased tasks and requirement references.

output_examples.input: Validate my generated planning documents before I give them to an AI coding agent.
output: A completeness review for required sections, user stories, acceptance criteria, and task structure.
Warnings for missing requirement links, missing components, weak scope boundaries, or unresolved placeholders.

output_examples.input: Create a design plan for a trading automation prototype.
output: A planning package that covers market data, order flow, risk checks, monitoring, and audit requirements.
A task breakdown that separates infrastructure, integrations, business logic, testing, and deployment.

best_practices: Provide target users, core features, integrations, constraints, and success metrics before generating documents.
Review every placeholder and acceptance criterion before implementation begins.
Write generated files to a dedicated planning directory to avoid overwriting existing documents.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated templates as final specifications without human review.
Do not mix requirements, design decisions, and tasks without traceability links.
Do not run validation against files that contain secrets or unrelated private content.

faq.question: What documents does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates requirements, system design, and implementation plan documents in Markdown format.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The planning outputs are plain Markdown and can guide these AI coding tools.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready architecture?
faq.answer: No. It creates structured drafts that need review by the project team.

faq.question: Does the skill require network access?
faq.answer: No runtime network access was found. URLs appear as metadata or documentation examples.

faq.question: What should users watch for when running the scripts?
faq.answer: The generator writes files named requirements.md, design.md, and tasks.md in the selected output directory.

faq.question: What does the validator check?
faq.answer: It checks document structure, required sections, placeholders, traceability, components, and task consistency.
